name: Pocket Proof
tagline: Turns daily chaos into shareable proof
description: Pocket Proof is a utility app that creates time-stamped, location-aware evidence packs from everyday moments that are easy to forget but hard to prove. It solves the common problem of needing to document things like appliance damage before a move, a noisy neighbor complaint, a wrong food delivery, or a workplace incident without fumbling through photos, notes, and screenshots scattered across different apps. Users can record a quick incident, attach media, and the app automatically organizes the details into a clean timeline with context such as time, place, weather, device metadata, and witness notes. This helps people build credible records in minutes instead of scrambling later when insurance claims, landlord disputes, warranty issues, or customer service escalations happen. A renter can document a broken sink with a few taps, a parent can log repeated school pickup delays, or a freelancer can preserve evidence of a missed client handoff. What makes Pocket Proof unique is that it turns messy everyday proof into a structured, exportable evidence packet designed for real-world resolution, not just storage.
features: The app captures incidents in a guided flow that prompts users for the right details at the moment they matter, so important context is not forgotten later.
It automatically stamps entries with time, GPS location, weather, and device metadata to strengthen the credibility and usefulness of each record.
Users can bundle photos, videos, voice notes, receipts, and messages into a single evidence pack that is easy to review, search, and share.
A timeline view organizes incidents chronologically and highlights patterns, which helps users prove repeated problems instead of relying on isolated screenshots.
One-tap export generates a professional PDF or share link tailored for landlords, insurers, HR teams, or customer support, making escalation faster and clearer.
audience: Adults aged 18-55 who frequently deal with rentals, warranties, service disputes, workplace issues, or family logistics. It is especially useful for urban renters, gig workers, freelancers, and anyone who needs quick, reliable documentation on the go.
useCases: Mia just discovered water leaking under her kitchen sink and wants to document the damage before emailing her landlord, so she opens Pocket Proof to capture photos, a voice note, and the exact time of discovery.

Jordan received the wrong replacement part for an appliance and needs proof for a warranty claim, so he uses the app to package the order receipt, unboxing video, and a labeled incident report in one link.
Elena had a recurring issue with a contractor arriving hours late and wants to track the pattern, so she logs each missed appointment and later exports a timeline to support a complaint.
